user_outcome: An operator's sworn maintainability cutover checks ordinary/post-sync rollback equality and one canonical readiness predicate, distinguishing legitimate deferrals and failing closed before the unavailable activation transaction.
rationale: C-09 is the one fail-closed readiness decision consumed by every later integration, release, and shipping adapter. It combines current lifecycle authority with exact rollback tree equality so raw deferred state can never bypass failed evidence.
in_scope: Derive the ordinary rollback envelope as every authored path in first-parent non-merge semantic history from the failed original's immutable start_commit through the verified rollback slice's pinned maintainability.implementation_head, including original, gap, rollback-authored, generated, and lock paths; exclude only the physical release root and recognized merge-only contributions, and never subtract later active authority.

Require every ordinary rollback envelope path's mode and object identity, including absence, to equal the original start_commit tree at the verified rollback head; unexpected later ordinary authority is a provenance failure requiring reconstruction rather than an envelope carve-out.

For a deterministic S11 post-sync invalidation, use start_commit through invalidated_review_head as the complete candidate envelope and the recognized synchronization merge's exact parent-2 tree as baseline, preserving later disjoint authoritative and sibling paths and requiring track reconstruction for later overlap.

Implement C-09 as one pure integration-ready predicate: verified or shipped requires current-cycle authoritative Verifier PASS and valid reports; an unstarted deferral requires null start, the empty pending cycle-0 lifecycle, and a Rule-2-complete deferral; a terminal re_slice_required original requires its recorded rollback to be verified or shipped and tree-equal under the applicable rule.
Reject raw deferred, superseded, missing rollback links, deferred rollback slices, invalid report or protocol authority, ambiguous provenance, or any inability to evaluate equality.

Introduce the already-planned sworn maintainability cutover spelling through the real command scaffold; it evaluates C-09 for the terminal bootstrap set, then exits non-zero with redacted reason cutover_transaction_unavailable until S13 lands, with no protocol-marker, ref, status, or index mutation.
out_of_scope: Choosing re_slice versus resume_in_scope and creating rollback or replacement slices belong to the Coach and Planner surfaces implemented in S13 and S17.
Performing a track, release, or ship transition belongs to S15-unified-track-merge and S16-release-ship-transitions.
The predicate does not repair, reset, synthesize, or narrow lifecycle or report history.
Deployment and journey-attestation checks remain outside C-09.

effort_complexity.rationale: Rollback has two baselines and must preserve disjoint later history while the readiness predicate closes every lifecycle and deferral bypass.

risks.description: A rollback-authored or otherwise surviving path escapes comparison because the envelope stops at the failed review head or subtracts later ordinary authority.
risks.mitigation: Enumerate every first-parent non-merge authored path through the verified rollback implementation head, exclude only release records and recognized merge-only contributions, and require exact start-tree equality; keep the parent-2 post-sync rule separate.

risks.description: A displayed deferred state becomes a permissive substitute for valid terminal lifecycle evidence.
risks.mitigation: Expose one C-09 predicate with three exhaustive success forms and fail every other state or unevaluable dependency closed.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN an ordinary re_slice_required original records a verified rollback THE SYSTEM SHALL enumerate every first-parent non-merge authored path from original start_commit through the rollback slice's pinned maintainability.implementation_head, including rollback-authored/generated/lock paths and gaps, exclude only the physical release root and recognized merge-only contributions, and report PASS only when every mode/object/absence equals the original start tree; unexpected later ordinary history or authored/merge overlap SHALL require reconstruction (C-04, C-06, C-08, C-09, N-04, N-06, N-07, N-09).

WHEN S11 records a recognized post-sync invalidation and the verified rollback restores the complete start_commit..invalidated_review_head candidate set to invalidating_sync_merge parent-2 while later authoritative intervals are disjoint THE SYSTEM SHALL report equality PASS and preserve every sibling and later path byte-for-byte (C-08, C-09, N-07).

IF any rollback envelope path—including a path first authored by the rollback slice—has a different mode or object ID from its canonical baseline, an expected absence is present, an expected path is absent, provenance is unrecognized, ordinary history unexpectedly introduces later authority, or a post-sync later authoritative interval overlaps THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return C-09 not-ready with exact identity evidence and SHALL NOT mutate a ref, status, index, or worktree (C-08, C-09, N-07, N-09).

WHEN C-09 evaluates a slice that is verified or shipped with a current-cycle authoritative Verifier PASS and valid C-03 through C-08 evidence THE SYSTEM SHALL return integration-ready; IF the authoritative PASS is absent, stale, rewritten, from another cycle, or its implementation head is no longer active THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return not-ready (C-03, C-05, C-06, C-08, C-09, N-09).

WHEN C-09 evaluates an unstarted planned-intent deferral with start_commit null, empty actual files, pending verification, the exact empty pending cycle-0 lifecycle, and at least one Rule-2-complete deferral THE SYSTEM SHALL return integration-ready; IF any execution evidence exists or the deferral lacks why, tracking, acknowledgement, or acknowledged_by THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return not-ready (C-06, C-09, N-09).

WHEN C-09 evaluates a deferred terminal re_slice_required original whose immutable rollback_slice_id resolves to a verified or shipped tree-equal rollback THE SYSTEM SHALL return integration-ready; IF the link is missing or changed, the rollback is deferred, non-terminal, not tree-equal, or raw state is superseded or deferred without either legal form THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return not-ready (C-06, C-09, N-07, N-09).

WHEN the built sworn binary runs maintainability cutover against terminal bootstrap records satisfying C-03 through C-09 THE SYSTEM SHALL reach the canonical readiness predicate through the public command, then exit non-zero with redacted reason cutover_transaction_unavailable and leave protocol marker, refs, statuses, board, and index unchanged; any stale PASS, illegal deferral, rollback inequality, or unevaluable evidence SHALL fail at C-09 instead (C-09, N-04, N-06, N-07, N-09).
